messages.text: Bassanio decided to select the lead casket because its modest warning sounded truer than gold or silver praise. Portia could barely stand still.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: You that choose not by the view, let me find fair fortune here.
He opened it and found Portia's portrait, painted so vividly that even the eyes seemed ready to speak.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: This portrait is less than the living original, yet it is enough to make me blessed.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Then I am yours, with this house, these servants, and myself. I accept your love and give you this ring.
The ring became a ceremony in miniature. She asked him to keep it as the sign of honor between them.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: If you part with it, lose it, or give it away, let that mean your love has failed.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: When this ring leaves my finger, let life leave my body.
Gratiano then confessed that he loved Nerissa, and Nerissa admitted that his suit had succeeded if Bassanio succeeded. Joy doubled itself.

messages.name: Gratiano
messages.text: Our wedding can follow yours. I kept time with my lord in love as well as in travel.
The celebration ended when Lorenzo, Jessica, and Salerio arrived with an urgent letter from Antonio. Bassanio read it and lost color.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: What loss can turn you so pale on the day of your happiness?

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: The dearest friend I have is ruined for my sake. His ships are lost, and Shylock demands the bond.
Antonio's letter did not blame him. It asked only to see Bassanio before death if friendship allowed.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: Every word opens a wound. I borrowed in my name, but Antonio gave his flesh as security.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Pay the debt many times over. Go to Venice with speed. A friend who bears such risk must not wait.
She turned private love into public duty without hesitation. The wedding would be brief, the separation immediate.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Marry me now, then go. I will not sleep in my full joy while your friend stands under a knife.
Bassanio accepted the command. His honor now required both gratitude to Portia and rescue for Antonio.
Before night ended, Belmont changed from a house of music to a station of departure, and the urgent letter ruled them all.
The portrait did not flatter Portia into a goddess. It caught something human in her face, and that made Bassanio trust the modest box more deeply.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: You see me happy, but also afraid. Accept both, or you do not accept me truly.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: I accept the lady, the fear, the fortune, and the honor of serving them all.
For a moment Belmont seemed secure. The ceremony of rings and vows made time slow enough for joy to gather.
Then Antonio's letter broke the music. Its urgent kindness hurt more than accusation because it refused to make Bassanio smaller.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: A love that cannot answer a friend's loss would be a poor beginning. Go quickly, and take my wealth with my trust.
Portia moved quickly because love had made Bassanio's danger her own. She did not pause to measure whether Antonio belonged to her household.

messages.name: Portia
messages.text: Any friend who signs flesh for you enters my care as well.
Bassanio left Belmont married, frightened, and newly aware that honor can demand departure at the very moment happiness begins.
Portia did not treat wealth as decoration. Once Antonio needed help, her fortune became movement: horses, money, letters, and immediate command.

messages.name: Bassanio
messages.text: I came to win a wife and found a partner stronger than my fear.
That knowledge made leaving both easier and more painful.
